response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced full-stack and web engineer with over 20 years of broad software development, but he is a poor fit for this role on nearly every critical dimension. The role requires native-level Japanese proficiency, which is entirely absent from his profile. His security domain knowledge — MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, embedded attack/tamper detection — is nonexistent. His C/C++ usage is peripheral and dated (NI LabWindows 2007–2008, a bachelor thesis project), while his primary expertise is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang on web and cloud platforms. There is no evidence of Linux kernel work, embedded firmware development, or LAN-switch/networking experience.
strengths: 20+ years of professional software development across diverse domains and languages, demonstrating adaptability
Early C/C++ experience at National Instruments on a real engineering IDE product provides a thin relevant foundation
Broad Linux familiarity across multiple projects and roles, including CI/CD and containerized infrastructure
concerns: Japanese language proficiency is entirely absent — this is listed as a hard requirement and is an immediate disqualifier
Zero experience with any of the required security technologies: MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption, or device attack/tamper detection
No embedded systems, kernel-level Linux, or networking (L2/L3, switch, SNMP, NETCONF) experience — career is exclusively web, cloud, and application-layer software
